_meta.issue: #124 [M16] Formative path S4 — skills and measuring impact (training module, rubric interaction)
_meta.interaction: Workflow-maturity scoring by rubric (kind: rubric) — formative exercise, NOT scored toward completion (no rubricId/threshold)
_meta.source: Holak Scale — two critical maturity thresholds: 8→9 and 10→11; skills and measuring impact. Attribution: quality-blog.eu/pl/blog/skala-holaka.
_meta.dataPolicy: ALL examples SYNTHETIC and generic (no real data/PII). Formative module — no pass threshold, no certificate.

intro: The hardest part is not the first levels but two thresholds: 8→9 (from individual good practice to wiring AI into a process) and 10→11 (from one-off improvements to continuous improvement). The test of maturity is a concrete, repeatable workflow (a skill): does it have fixed context, verification, reversibility, and measured impact. This module teaches you to score a skill's maturity against a rubric. It is a growth exercise, not a test.

blocks.text: Many people and teams stop just before level 9 or 11. The 8→9 threshold is when one person's good practice becomes part of a process: a clear assistant/decision-maker split, reversibility and an audit trail. The 10→11 threshold is when improvements turn into a routine of measurement, calibration and continuous improvement.

items: 8→9: individual practice → a process with control and a trail.
10→11: one-off improvements → a measured, continuously calibrated system.
At both thresholds the key is not a new tool but a new working habit.

blocks.title: Holak Scale
blocks.text: The 8→9 and 10→11 thresholds are the most common places AI adoption gets stuck.

screens.title: A skill as a maturity sample

blocks.text: Maturity is best seen on a concrete, repeatable workflow (a skill). An immature skill is a „quick prompt”: shifting context, no verification, no trail, success measured by impression. A mature skill has fixed context and format, an explicit verification step, reversibility with a trail, and measured impact on quality and time.

blocks.title: A stall signal
blocks.text: If you use AI smoothly but do not measure its impact and do not calibrate how you judge output — you are probably at 10, not 11.

Below you'll score an example skill's maturity against four criteria. In each criterion, check the traits that indicate maturity — checking everything (including distractors) does not satisfy the criterion. No pass threshold: the feedback shows what is missing to reach a higher level.

interaction.title: Score the maturity of an AI workflow (a skill)
interaction.intro: For each criterion, check the traits that indicate a mature skill. This is a growth exercise — no pass threshold; the feedback points out what is missing.

criteria.name: Context and repeatability
criteria.prompt: What indicates mature context management in this skill?
criteria.hint: Maturity is fixed, selected context — not describing from scratch every time.

options.text: Fixed, deliberate context and output format.
Context described from scratch, in different words, every time.
Selecting only the data relevant to the task.

criteria.name: Verifiability
criteria.prompt: What makes the skill verifiable before the result is used?
criteria.hint: The model's confident tone is not proof — an explicit check step is needed.

options.text: An explicit step to check the output against a source.

criteria.name: Reversibility and trail
criteria.hint: Threshold 8→9: control and a trail instead of irreversible actions without approval.

options.text: Actions leave a trail and can be undone.
Irreversible decisions executed without human approval.

criteria.name: Measuring impact
criteria.prompt: What separates level 11 (continuous improvement) from merely using AI?
criteria.hint: Threshold 10→11: measured impact and calibration, not the mere fact of use.

options.text: Measured impact on quality and time, and adjusting the approach.
What counts is the mere fact that we use AI.

points: Maturity shows on a concrete skill: fixed context, verification, reversibility with a trail, measured impact.
Threshold 8→9: turn individual practice into a process with control and a trail.
Threshold 10→11: turn improvements into a routine of measurement, calibration and continuous improvement.
checklist: Does my most frequent skill have fixed context and output format?
Does it have an explicit verification step and leave a trail?
Do I measure its impact on quality and time, or just use it?
summary.nextStep: Next step: take one of your skills and add the missing criterion with the lowest score — most often it is the verification step or measuring impact.
